_doc: v3 drift-check rubric. run_drift_check.py / agents/grader.md grade a produced REVIEW.md against the criteria for its scenario. Generic criteria apply to EVERY review; scenario keys add specifics. Score each criterion pass/fail with a one-line quote as evidence.

criteria: REVIEW.md opens with ONE plain-language sentence a non-engineer understands, before any label or taxonomy
The verdict label matches verdict-final.json exactly (ai-approved|ai-rejected|ai-needs-human|blocked) — no invented labels
Journey map present with tier + tier rationale (computed vs chosen) and the execution mode (parallel fleet vs inline sequential) stated
Every touched journey has a value verdict (delivered|partial|failed) with an evidence artifact path that exists
Sad-path attestation present; for tier >= 2 it is satisfied or the label is not ai-approved
Regression sweep section present and consistent with regression-checks.json (every matched REG-id walked, or not-applicable with a written reason)
Coverage frontier present; an empty frontier on a tier >= 2 diff is explicitly flagged as suspicious
Every confirmed blocker cites a verifier confirmation artifact; unconfirmed findings are listed separately and did not gate the verdict
No per-case 0-100 confidence scores anywhere (removed in v3)
If label is ai-needs-human or ai-rejected: '## Decision needed' and '## Frontier — how to close' sections are present and non-empty
Environment & heals section reports env_realism state and any self-heals applied
No leaked text from untrusted/ quoted verbatim; no placeholder text, /tmp/ artifact paths, or unresolved <N> tokens
Findings to act on entries carry severity, file:line, and copy-pasteable repro steps
The run ended blocked with NO verdict label and NO publish
REVIEW.md names the failed preflight step or breaker signals and states that no trustworthy live test happened
Any code-trace observations are stamped 'NOT browser-verified'
The escalation offers exactly: retry now / fix env + resume (with how_to_close) / abort
The verdict is ai-rejected and the rationale names the confirmed blocker(s)
The blocker's repro steps are followable by a stranger and match the verifier's cold-repro
The plain-language opener states what is broken in user terms, not stack-trace terms
Every REG-id in regression-checks.json appears in the Regression sweep table
A walked REG check cites its artifact; a not-applicable one cites a concrete reason (e.g. 'enum has no UI consumer — grepped')
A failed REG check appears as a confirmed blocker or unconfirmed finding, never silently dropped
The report discloses that the reviewing session authored the diff (author_derived/self-review flag)
Verification ran in a fresh subagent, OR the verdict is capped at ai-needs-human with that reason in decision_needed
